result:
Implemented contextual search snippets.

In [main.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main.go), `searchNotes` now reads parsed note content, matches against `title + body`, and prints a fifth tab-separated column with a snippet from the note body. The snippet logic prefers the first body line containing the query, trims and normalizes whitespace, clips long lines around the match, and falls back to the first non-empty body line or `-` if the body is empty. Because it uses parsed note bodies, metadata headers like `Tags:` and `Archived:` are excluded from snippets.

In [main_test.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main_test.go), I updated search tests to assert body excerpts appear in output and added coverage proving snippets come from the parsed body rather than note metadata.

I could not run `gofmt` or `go test` in this environment because neither `gofmt` nor `go` is installed.
